Understanding NIPT

A Blood Test That Screens
Your Baby's Chromosomes

NIPT analyses cell-free fetal DNA (cffDNA) present in the mother's bloodstream. This DNA originates from the placenta and can be detected reliably from Week 10 of pregnancy using Next Generation Sequencing (NGS) technology.

Unlike invasive procedures such as amniocentesis or CVS, NIPT carries zero risk of miscarriage. Only a standard maternal blood draw of less than 5ml is required. With NIFTY™ technology, accuracy exceeds 99% for the most common chromosomal conditions.

NIPT is a screening test — not a diagnostic one. A positive result indicates increased risk and should always be confirmed with diagnostic testing under physician guidance.

🧬

Trisomy 21

Down Syndrome — affects 1 in 700–750 pregnancies.

🔬

Trisomy 18

Edwards Syndrome — affects 1 in 6,000.

🧪

Trisomy 13

Patau Syndrome — affects 1 in 10,000–20,000.

🩺

Sex Chromosomes

Turner, Klinefelter, Triple X and XYY.

✦

Microdeletions

DiGeorge, Prader-Willi, Angelman and others.

👶

Gender (Optional)

Fetal sex from Week 10.

Timing

When to Get Tested

10

From Week 10

NIPT can be performed from Week 10 — the earliest prenatal genetic screening method available.

Earliest Window
12

Weeks 11–13

Optimal window — can be combined with first-trimester NT ultrasound for a comprehensive picture.

Optimal Window
20+

Anytime During Pregnancy

NIPT can be done at any gestational age — earlier results allow more time for informed decision-making.

Anytime
Safety & Reassurance

Zero Risk to
Mother or Baby

NIPT is a screening test. A positive result indicates increased risk — not certainty — and must be confirmed by a licensed physician or genetic counsellor.

  • 💉

    Simple Blood Draw Only

    Only a standard maternal blood draw of less than 5ml required. No risk to the baby.

  • 📋

    Pre-Test Information

    Our team ensures every patient understands scope, limitations, and next steps before testing.

  • 🏥

    Post-Result Support

    Our team coordinates with OB-GYN specialists for follow-up in positive or inconclusive cases.

  • 🔒

    Confidential Results

    Results shared only with the patient. Shared reports with written consent only.

🤰

Who Should Consider NIPT?

Recommended for all pregnant women — especially those aged 35+, with a prior chromosomal abnormality, abnormal conventional screening results, IVF-conceived pregnancies, or who wish to avoid invasive testing. Suitable for twin pregnancies.

Frequently Asked

Your Questions, Answered.

QWhen can NIPT be done?

From Week 10 of pregnancy onward. Earlier testing gives earlier reassurance.

QIs NIPT safe for the baby?

Yes — completely safe. Only a simple blood draw from the mother is required.

QWhat conditions does NIPT screen for?

Primarily Trisomy 21, 18 and 13. Advanced panels also screen for selected additional chromosomal conditions.

QHow long does it take to get results?

Results are generally available within 10–14 working days.

QCan NIPT determine gender?

Yes — available as an optional add-on in selected panels from Week 10.

QIs home collection available for NIPT?

Yes. Home collection is available across Abu Dhabi.

Give Your Baby the Benefit of
Early Knowing

Book your NIPT today. From Week 10 of pregnancy. No referral required. Home collection available.
